body_txt: The Satisfied Street Rat is a character archetype common to more cynical works that has the following characteristics:  Frequently Anti Heroes , and often Byronic Heroes who embodies on being Just a Gangster and proud. The degree to which the character is well-adjusted versus harboring a lot of pain inside depends on the writer. See also Wrong Side of the Tracks , Neighbourhood-Friendly Gangsters , and Damn, It Feels Good to Be a Gangster! As children, they are more likely to have been The Artful Dodger rather than the Street Urchin , but the Dodger is more of a trickster where the Street Rat is more of a cutthroat. May have been raised by The Fagin . Those who knew the Street Rat as a more vulnerable child may see them as a case of From Nobody to Nightmare in adulthood. Compare Had to Be Sharp .
